sections.heading: 'Get' as become
sections.body: 'Get' + adjective means to become: get tired, get angry, get better, get worse, get married, get dressed, get lost, get stuck. This is extremely common and more natural than 'become' in spoken English.

sections.heading: 'Get' as obtain/receive
sections.body: 'Get' + noun can mean receive or obtain: get a call, get a message, get a raise, get a job, get the point, get a degree, get permission.

sections.heading: 'Get' as arrive/reach
sections.body: 'Get' + place or 'get to' + place means arrive: 'When did you get home?' 'I got to the office late.' 'Get there early.'

examples.english: She got a promotion last month.
What time did you get home?

exercises.instruction: Replace the underlined word with the correct form of 'get'.

quiz.question: 'Get' + adjective most closely means:
quiz.explanation: 'Get' + adjective describes a transition into a new state — it means 'become'.
